def predict_next_frame(frame, action, xy=None):
    """
    Deterministic predictor based on the exact transitions listed in the prompt.
    It contains a hard‑coded mapping from (action, optional coordinates) to the
    list of cells that must be updated and their new values.
    """
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Mapping: (action, xy) -> [(row, col, new_value), ...]
    # For actions other than ACTION6 the second element of the key is None.
    # Only the transitions that appear in the prompt are listed here.
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    _MAP = {
        ("ACTION4", None): [
            (63, 45, 0)                     # clear bottom‑row cell
        ],
        ("ACTION6", (19, 22)): [
            (63, 56, 0)
        ],
        ("ACTION6", (20, 54)): [
            (63, 54, 0)
        ],
        ("ACTION6", (25, 0)): [
            (63, 15, 0)
        ],
        ("ACTION6", (3, 36)): [
            (63, 21, 0)
        ],
        ("ACTION6", (17, 39)): [
            (63, 7, 0)
        ],
        ("ACTION2", None): [                # pattern swapping a 3×3 block
            (24, 9, 1), (24,10,1),(24,11,1),
            (25, 9,1),(25,10,1),(25,11,1),
            (26, 9,1),(26,10,1),(26,11,1),
            (27, 9,14),(27,10,14),(27,11,14),
            (28, 9,14),(28,10,0),(28,11,14),
            (29, 9,14),(29,10,14),(29,11,14),
            (63,24,0)
        ],
        ("ACTION2", "single"): [           # single bottom‑row cell
            (63,53,0)
        ],
        ("ACTION2", "block21"): [          # block at rows 33‑38, cols 21‑23
            (33,21,1),(33,22,1),(33,23,1),
            (34,21,1),(34,22,1),(34,23,1),
            (35,21,1),(35,22,1),(35,23,1),
            (36,21,14),(36,22,14),(36,23,14),
            (37,21,14),(37,22,0),(37,23,14),
            (38,21,14),(38,22,14),(38,23,14)
        ],
        ("ACTION2", "block18"): [          # block at rows 33‑38, cols 18‑20
            (33,18,1),(33,19,1),(33,20,1),
            (34,18,1),(34,19,1),(34,20,1),
            (35,18,1),(35,19,1),(35,20,1),
            (36,18,14),(36,19,14),(36,20,14),
            (37,18,14),(37,19,0),(37,20,14),
            (38,18,14),(38,19,14),(38,20,14),
            (63,47,0)
        ],
        ("ACTION3", None): [                # pattern with rows 33‑35, cols 15‑20
            (33,15,14),(33,16,1),(33,17,1),
            (33,18,14),(33,19,14),(33,20,14),
            (34,15,14),(34,16,1),(34,17,1),
            (34,18,14),(34,19,0),(34,20,14),
            (35,15,14),(35,16,1),(35,17,1),
            (35,18,14),(35,19,14),(35,20,14)
        ],
        ("ACTION3", "alt"): [               # alternative pattern rows 30‑32
            (30,18,1),(30,19,1),(30,20,1),
            (30,21,14),(30,22,14),(30,23,14),
            (31,18,1),(31,19,1),(31,20,1),
            (31,21,14),(31,22,0),(31,23,14),
            (32,18,1),(32,19,1),(32,20,1),
            (32,21,14),(32,22,14),(32,23,14)
        ],
    }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Choose the correct key.
    # For ACTION2 we need to decide which block is present by inspecting a
    # single cell that uniquely identifies each pattern.
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    if action == "ACTION6":
        key = ("ACTION6", xy)
    elif action == "ACTION4":
        key = ("ACTION4", None)
    elif action == "ACTION2":
        # decide which variant based on a distinguishing cell
        if frame[24][9] == 14:          # large 3×3 block pattern
            key = ("ACTION2", None)
        elif frame[33][21] == 14:      # block at cols 21‑23
            key = ("ACTION2", "block21")
        elif frame[33][18] == 14:      # block at cols 18‑20
            key = ("ACTION2", "block18")
        else:                           # single bottom‑row cell
            key = ("ACTION2", "single")
    elif action == "ACTION3":
        # distinguish by the value at (33,15)
        if frame[33][15] == 4:
            key = ("ACTION3", None)
        else:
            key = ("ACTION3", "alt")
    else:
        # For any other action not present in the mapping return unchanged.
        return [row[:] for row in frame]

    changes = _MAP.get(key, [])
    new_frame = [row[:] for row in frame]
    for r, c, v in changes:
        if 0 <= r < 64 and 0 <= c < 64:
            new_frame[r][c] = v
    return new_frame
